summary: This is a brief link post where Spolsky promotes two other pieces of content. He teases his 'Strategy Letter I' which categorizes startups into two types — 'Ben and Jerry's' companies versus 'Amazon' companies — and warns that not knowing which type you are leads to strategic mistakes. He also links to a reader suggestion that he finds deceptively appealing but potentially problematic.
key_points: Startups fall into two fundamentally different categories that require different strategies
Misidentifying your startup type leads to compounding strategic errors
Ideas that sound good at first glance can turn out to be minefields upon reflection
money_quotes: If you don't know which one you are, you're going to make lots of mistakes and you won't even know why.
It sounds good at first, but after a minute you realize it might be a minefield.
key_insight: Startups must correctly identify their fundamental strategic type — growth-focused or profitability-focused — because the wrong self-diagnosis leads to invisible but compounding mistakes.
